summary: Added a partial C decompilation for the Flat Zone stage module, replacing an effectively empty grflatzone.c with stage data, callback tables, lifecycle callbacks, object spawning/control logic, random timing helpers, animation/map collision updates, and item/dynamic-attribute interactions. The PR also introduced grflatzone.h prototypes, added Flat Zone-specific GroundVars union members in gr/types.h, moved grDynamicAttr_UnkStruct earlier for reuse, and corrected mpLib_80051EC8's prototype to pointer-based arguments needed by the new call site.

key_files.role: Main partial Flat Zone decompilation; added StageCallbacks, StageData, many grFlatzone_80216E74-through-802182BC routines, random timing helpers, stage object setup, animation control, map collision refreshes, camera setup, and item/dynamic-attribute behavior.
New public header with address-commented prototypes for Flat Zone stage entry points and callbacks.
Added GroundVars_flatzone and GroundVars_flatzone2 layouts and inserted them into the GroundVars union; relocated grDynamicAttr_UnkStruct definition before those structs.
Updated mpLib_80051EC8 declaration from value-like unknown integer parameters to pointer parameters matching the Flat Zone collision-query call.

what_changed: Created the Flat Zone stage callback array grFz_803E7940 with nine StageCallbacks entries and wired it into StageData grFz_803E7A00 for /GrFz.dat.

Implemented the stage init path grFlatzone_80216E78, including Ground_801C49F8 parameter fetch, stage_info flag setup, spawning several ground object IDs through grFlatzone_80216F48, camera configuration, and blast-zone-bottom offset adjustment.

Added grFlatzone_80216F48 as a shared ground-object factory/setup routine using Ground_801C14D0, GET_GROUND, GObj_SetupGXLink, callback assignment, optional init callback execution, and optional HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54 registration.

Implemented many Flat Zone object callbacks that initialize JObjs, hide/show geometry, start grAnime_801C8138 animations, call Ground_801C2ED0/Ground_801C2FE0, coordinate other ground objects via Ground_801C2BA4, and maintain per-object state in gp->gv.flatzone or gp->gv.flatzone2.
Added random helper inlines rand_int_inner and rand_int plus SIGN_RANDOM macro to model randomized Flat Zone timing/direction behavior using HSD_Randi and HSD_Randf.

Added behavior for Flat Zone hazards/effects, including dynamic attribute creation through grDynamicAttr_801CA0F8, map collision probing via mpLib_80051EC8, map/collision refresh via mpLib_80058560, and item spawning through it_802EEFA8.
Added a static animation-selection table grFz_803E7A68 used by Flat Zone animation transition logic.
Added grflatzone.h prototypes for the newly introduced address-named functions, including grFlatzone_80218060, grFlatzone_802181B4, grFlatzone_80218260, grFlatzone_802182B4, and grFlatzone_802182BC.
Added GroundVars_flatzone and GroundVars_flatzone2 structs in gr/types.h, with fields at xC4/xC5/xC6/xC7/xC8/xCA/xCC/xCE/xD0/xD4 and a dynamic-attribute pointer field in flatzone2.
Changed mpLib_80051EC8's prototype so its second, third, and fourth parameters are s32*, s32*, and Vec3* rather than plain s32 values.
smart_moves: Centralized stage-object creation in grFlatzone_80216F48 instead of duplicating gobj setup across every callback entry; this mirrors the StageCallbacks table and keeps GX link/proc/callback wiring consistent.
Introduced dedicated Flat Zone GroundVars structs rather than relying only on raw gp->gv._ byte indexing, while still preserving raw access where the exact layout or meaning remained uncertain.
Kept address-based symbol names and address comments in the header, which is useful for partial decompilation where semantic names are not yet proven.
Used small inline helpers for random integer ranges and NULL-safe cross-object state pokes, likely to satisfy codegen constraints while making repeated matching patterns explicit.

Adjusted a shared library prototype only as far as the new evidence required: mpLib_80051EC8 now reflects pointer arguments observed at the Flat Zone call site without broad renaming or over-documentation.
decomp_lessons: For stage modules, start by reconstructing the StageCallbacks table and StageData; those tables reveal object count/order, entry-point addresses, and which routines are init/update/destruct-style callbacks.
When matching stage object state, adding per-stage GroundVars union variants can improve typed access and matching readability, but keep unknown x-offset field names until semantics are established.
A partial match can still be valuable if it lands stable headers, table wiring, and struct layout improvements, even when some routines remain address-named or not fully semantically understood.
Cross-object Flat Zone logic repeatedly uses Ground_801C2BA4 object IDs to coordinate stage hazards; future work should preserve object ID relationships when renaming or splitting behavior.
Map collision and dynamic attribute calls are often the evidence needed to correct shared prototypes; verify pointer/value parameters from concrete call sites before changing library headers.
Randomized stage behavior may require helper inlines/macros to match original ternary/range codegen instead of replacing it with cleaner but nonmatching abstractions.
naming_conventions: Stage-local data uses the grFz_ prefix, such as grFz_803E7940, grFz_803E7A00, grFz_803E7A68, grFz_804D6AB0, and grFz_803B8430.
Unidentified Flat Zone functions keep address-derived names of the form grFlatzone_80216E78 and fn_8021706C.
Header prototypes include address comments like /* 216E78 */ before each function declaration.
Unknown struct fields are named by offset, such as xC4, xC5, xC8, xCC, xD0, and xD4, or by unkN when representing stage parameter data.
The GroundVars union uses per-stage member names such as flatzone and flatzone2 alongside existing generic unk and other stage-specific variants.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used a StageCallbacks array with exact function-pointer ordering and flags, including a 0xC0000000 entry, to match stage callback table data.
Preserved explicit casts such as (HSD_GObj*) gobj and raw union/byte accesses gp->gv._[...] where needed for codegen or uncertain layout.
Used inline helper functions rand_int_inner, rand_int, and grFlatzone_802171D4_inline to express repeated logic while likely controlling generated code shape.
Used address-named static globals and extern declarations for unresolved cross-module symbols instead of forcing premature semantic names.
Left some evidence of partial matching in the source, including a large #if 0 block for grFlatzone_80218060 in the diff excerpt, indicating not all routines were cleanly matched in this PR.
Corrected mpLib_80051EC8 to accept pointer outputs, enabling the Flat Zone call passing &pos_2 and &ret to type-check and better reflect original ABI usage.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice, so there is no direct reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching grFlatzone_80218060, grFlatzone_802181B4, grFlatzone_80218260, grFlatzone_802182B4, or grFlatzone_802182BC.
Search for later renames of GroundVars_flatzone fields xC4/xC5/xC8/xCC/xD0/xD4 to semantic hazard or animation-state names.
Check whether the mpLib_80051EC8 prototype was further refined after this PR based on other call sites.
Compare Flat Zone stage callback-table reconstruction with other stage PRs that add StageCallbacks and StageData from empty stage modules.
Look for subsequent cleanup of raw gp->gv._ accesses in grflatzone.c into typed flatzone or flatzone2 fields.
